title.es: Black-Scholes y la Trinidad de la Incertidumbre

summary_es: Este modulo examina la formula de Black-Scholes-Merton, sus cinco supuestos criticos y por que cada uno falla en mercados financieros reales. Cada falla se mapea a uno de los tres tipos de incertidumbre: la aleatoria (retornos con colas pesadas en lugar de Gaussianos), la epistemica (volatilidad desconocida y cambiante, no constante) y la ontologica (saltos, cambios de regimen y eventos sin precedente). Se implementa BSM en Python, se construye un volatility smile sintetico usando simulacion Monte Carlo con Student-t, y se cuantifica como cada tipo de incertidumbre afecta el pricing de opciones. El modulo demuestra que BSM es un benchmark util pero peligroso si se usa sin entender sus limitaciones fundamentales.
summary_en: This module examines the Black-Scholes-Merton formula, its five critical assumptions, and why each fails in real financial markets. Each failure maps to one of three uncertainty types: aleatory (fat-tailed returns instead of Gaussian), epistemic (unknown and changing volatility rather than constant), and ontological (jumps, regime changes, and unprecedented events). BSM is implemented in Python, a synthetic volatility smile is built using Monte Carlo simulation with Student-t, and the pricing impact of each uncertainty type is quantified. The module demonstrates that BSM is a useful benchmark but dangerous if used without understanding its fundamental limitations.
learning_objectives_es: Implementar Black-Scholes para opciones europeas y verificar paridad put-call
Demostrar que retornos financieros tienen colas pesadas incompatibles con BSM
Construir un volatility smile sintetico con simulacion Monte Carlo Student-t
Clasificar fallas de BSM segun la trinidad de incertidumbre (aleatoria, epistemica, ontologica)
Cuantificar el impacto de cada tipo de incertidumbre en pricing de opciones
learning_objectives_en: Implement Black-Scholes for European options and verify put-call parity
Demonstrate that financial returns have fat tails incompatible with BSM
Build a synthetic volatility smile with Student-t Monte Carlo simulation
Classify BSM failures according to the uncertainty trinity (aleatory, epistemic, ontological)
Quantify the pricing impact of each uncertainty type on options

parameters.name: Uncertainty Trinity
parameters.origin_phrase: Three types of uncertainty: aleatory, epistemic, and ontological

parameters.name: Non-Gaussian Distributions
parameters.origin_phrase: Financial returns exhibit fat tails inconsistent with Gaussian assumptions

parameters.name: Model Specification Errors
parameters.origin_phrase: BSM's five assumptions are each a specification error

parameters.name: Model Robustness
parameters.origin_phrase: Robust models must account for fat tails, parameter uncertainty, and regime changes

parameters.name: Non-Probabilistic Risk Flaws
parameters.origin_phrase: BSM treats all uncertainty as aleatory, ignoring epistemic and ontological risks

parameters.name: Academic Arrogance
parameters.origin_phrase: LTCM's collapse demonstrated the danger of blind faith in elegant models
